overview.short: Rajiv Gandhi National Park is a national park located in Rameswaram of Kadapa District, Andhra Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Rajiv Gandhi National Park is a national park located in Rameswaram of Kadapa District, Andhra Pradesh, India. Its area is about 2.4 square kilometers of tropical dry deciduous forest mostly growing on a sandy soil. It lies on the north bank of the Penna River.

topPlaces.name: Proddatur
topPlaces.description: Proddatur is a town in the YSR Kadapa District of Andhra Pradesh, located on the banks of the Penna River. The city is a municipality, and also the mandal headquarters of Proddatur mandal.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Rajiv Gandhi National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Rajiv Gandhi National Park is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Rajiv Gandhi National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kadapa Airport (33 km) and the nearest railway station is Tirupati Railway Station (153 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Rajiv Gandhi National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Rajiv Gandhi National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Rajiv Gandhi National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Rajiv Gandhi National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
